task: I’ve got a short note to send about a delay on a project, and I want it to sound clean and professional without being wordy. Can you help me draft something brief that gets to the point and still feels respectful?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a brief, professional version you can use:

**Subject:** Project Delay Update

Hi [Name],  
I wanted to let you know the project is running behind schedule due to [brief reason]. I’m working to resolve the issue and will share an updated timeline as soon as possible. Thanks for your understanding.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it:
- more formal
- more apologetic
- more casual
- tailored to a client, manager, or team

user_simulator.text:
Pretty close. A little too generic though — feels like a template. I’d want it tighter and more specific-sounding, without the extra options tacked on.

Maybe something like:

Hi [Name],
The project is running behind due to [brief reason]. I’m working through it now and will send an updated timeline as soon as I can.

Best,
[Your Name]

If you want, give me one version that sounds a bit more direct, like something I’d actually send to a client.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a more direct client-facing version:

Hi [Name],  
The project is running behind due to [brief reason]. I’m working through it now and will send an updated timeline as soon as I can.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can make it even sharper or a little more polished depending on the tone you want.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Clean, direct, not padded out. That’s basically the tone I wanted.
